---
title: "Code organization and data flow"
toc: true
---

This page is the map a reader or contributor needs to navigate the `tslai`
source: how the modules fit together, what flows between them, and which
invariants keep the result correct. It documents the *code paths*, not the
science — the authoritative model and its justification live in
[`CLAUDE.md`](https://github.com/munch-group/tslai/blob/main/CLAUDE.md) (referenced
below as §N). Names here are verified against
[`src/tslai/__init__.py`](https://github.com/munch-group/tslai/blob/main/src/tslai/__init__.py);
links go to the [API reference](../api/index.qmd) where one exists.

## The one call

The whole library funnels through a single high-level entry point:

```python
import tslai

ts     = tslai.simulate_admixture(n_admix=10, n_ref=10)   # or tslai.io.infer_tree_sequence(...)
labels = {0: 0, 1: 0, 2: 1, 3: 1}                         # reference sample-node -> ancestry state
painting = tslai.paint(ts, labels)                        # EM-fit on references, paint the queries

painting.posteriors[q]            # soft per-position posterior for query q (list[Segment])
painting.segments(deadband=0.4)   # hard ancestry tracts for downstream dating
```

[`paint`](../api/paint.qmd) ties three stages together — **fit**, **paint**,
**segment** — and returns a [`Painting`](../api/Painting.qmd). Everything else in
the package is either machinery underneath those stages or tooling around them
(front ends, comparators, metrics, experiment drivers).

## End-to-end data flow

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  subgraph IN["Input front ends — tslai.io"]
    TSI["io_tsinfer<br/>infer_tree_sequence"]
    SNG["io_singer<br/>singer_tree_sequences"]
    REL["Relate --compress<br/>(external)"]
  end

  TS["tskit.TreeSequence<br/>(cross-tree node-ID stable)"]
  LBL["labels: dict<br/>ref node -> state"]

  TSI --> TS
  SNG --> TS
  REL --> TS
  TS --> PAINT
  LBL --> PAINT

  subgraph PAINT["paint() — api.py"]
    direction TB

    subgraph FIT["fit() — em.py  (blocked EM loop)"]
      direction TB
      EMIT["build_emissions<br/>soft clamp + query"]
      ACC["accumulate.py<br/>edge_diffs sweep,<br/>bank per edge × span"]
      PRU["pruning.py<br/>up/down per root<br/>(γ, ξ, loo, loglik)"]
      VL["branch_stats.py<br/>Van Loan rewards<br/>(dwell, jumps)"]
      MSTEP["M-step (em.py)<br/>m_step_Q / _pi / _w"]
      EMIT --> ACC
      ACC --> PRU
      ACC --> VL
      PRU --> ACC
      VL --> ACC
      ACC --> MSTEP
      MSTEP -->|next iter| EMIT
    end

    POST["posterior_table — output.py<br/>down-pass γ per query<br/>-> Segment tracks"]
    SMOOTH["bp/horizontal.py<br/>(optional smooth=True)"]

    FIT -->|"Q, π, w"| POST
    POST --> SMOOTH
  end

  PAINT --> PAINTING["Painting<br/>.posteriors (soft)"]
  PAINTING --> SEG[".segments(deadband)<br/>output.hard_segments<br/>(hard tracts)"]
  PAINTING --> METRICS["tslai.metrics / tslai.compare<br/>(scoring vs. truth)"]
```

### Walkthrough

**0. Get a tree sequence.** `tslai` paints *any* `tskit.TreeSequence` whose nodes
keep stable IDs across marginal trees (§5). The
[`tslai.io`](../api/io.infer_tree_sequence.qmd) namespace gathers the supported
ways to obtain one from genotypes; a true/simulated ARG works directly.

**1. Emissions (§2.2).**
[`build_emissions`](../api/em.build_emissions.qmd) (in `em.py`) turns the labels
into per-sample Felsenstein emission vectors. A labelled reference tip `i` gets the
**soft clamp** `w_i · 1[label] + (1 − w_i) · π` (anchors default to `w = 1`, a hard
clamp); every other sample gets the **query** emission `π` (flat / root-frequency).
This is rebuilt each EM iteration because `w` and `π` move.

**2. E-step: pruning, per tree, per root (§3.1).**
[`pruning.py`](../api/pruning.prune_tree.qmd) runs exact two-pass sum-product
message passing on each marginal tree. The up-pass (post-order) accumulates partial
likelihoods `L_u(s)` as a product over **all** children (polytomy-safe); the
down-pass (pre-order) yields the posterior marginal `γ_u`, the joint parent–child
posterior `ξ_{(p,c)}` (a `K×K` object per branch), a **leave-one-out** marginal
`loo_u` (the outside message excluding a tip's own emission — credibility evidence
that cannot self-confirm), and the exact tree log-likelihood. A marginal tree may
be a **forest**, so this runs once *per root* and aggregates.

**3. Edge-blocked, span-weighted sufficient statistics (§3.3 — the correctness
core).** [`accumulate.py`](../api/accumulate.accumulate_sufficient_statistics.qmd)
drives the genome with `zip(ts.edge_diffs(), ts.trees())`. For each marginal tree
it prunes once, then **banks each entering edge's contribution exactly once, on
entry, weighted by that edge's own span** `(right − left)`. A clade persisting
across many trees is one set of wide-span edges, so summing by span partitions the
genome without double-counting (the edge-table invariant: the intervals on which a
node is a *child* are disjoint). Root-state mass is accumulated *per interval*, not
per edge. The accumulators are `S_dwell`, `S_jumps`, `S_root`, per-tip `S_cred`
(agree/disagree for the credibility update), and a span-integrated `loglik`.

**4. Per-branch Van Loan rewards (§3.2).** For each entering edge,
[`branch_stats.py`](../api/branch_stats.branch_expected_stats.qmd) computes the
endpoint-conditioned **expected dwell time per state** and **expected jump counts
per ordered pair** via the Van Loan block-triangular matrix exponential
([Van Loan, 1978](https://doi.org/10.1109/TAC.1978.1101743);
[Hobolth and Jensen, 2011](https://doi.org/10.1239/jap/1324046009)). The
`(Q, t)`-dependent part (`branch_kernel`) is **cached by branch length** and applied
to each edge's `ξ` cheaply (`stats_from_kernel`), so the expensive `expm` runs once
per *distinct* branch length per iteration, not once per edge. This module is the
designated **Phasic seam** (§12): swap the `expm` blocks for Phasic without changing
callers.

**5. M-step (closed form, §3.4).** Back in `em.py`, the pooled statistics give the
generator [`m_step_Q`](../api/em.m_step_Q.qmd) (`q_mn = S_jumps[m,n] / S_dwell[m]`),
the root frequencies [`m_step_pi`](../api/em.m_step_pi.qmd), and the per-tip
credibility [`m_step_w`](../api/em.m_step_w.qmd) (MAP under a `Beta(α, β)` prior).
[`fit`](../api/fit.qmd) iterates steps 1–5 to a log-likelihood tolerance or
`max_iter`, returning a [`FitResult`](../api/FitResult.qmd) `(Q, π, {w_i},
loglik_history)`. The default `estimate_pi=False` holds `π` uniform — the robust
choice, since `π` is a prior on the arbitrary GMRCA state and estimating it from
washing deep branches is the §6 degeneracy.

**6. Deliverable: the soft painting (§2.4).** With the fitted `(Q, π, w)`, `paint`
rebuilds emissions and calls
[`posterior_table`](../api/output.posterior_table.qmd) (in `output.py`), which runs
the down-pass per marginal tree and records each query's posterior as contiguous
[`Segment`](../api/output.Segment.qmd)s covering `[0, L)`. Spans where a sample is
an isolated root are tagged `MISSING_INFO` (the tree carries *no information* — the
posterior is the prior `π`, distinct from a 50–50 *uncertain* call). The result is
wrapped in a [`Painting`](../api/Painting.qmd).

**7. Optional horizontal smoothing (§7).** With `paint(..., smooth=True)`,
[`bp/horizontal.py`](../api/bp.bp_smooth_track.qmd) runs a single-pass genome-axis
forward–backward (EP approximation) over each tip's per-tree beliefs, with a
per-breakpoint near-identity transition (switch penalty `epsilon`). This adds the
propagation of *uncertainty* across persist-but-reparent breakpoints that blocked EM
structurally omits — **recommended on inferred (tsinfer / Relate) ARGs**, redundant
on a true ARG.

**8. Hard tracts.** [`Painting.segments(deadband)`](../api/Painting.qmd) collapses
the soft posteriors into hard `(left, right, state)` tracts via
[`hard_segments`](../api/output.hard_segments.qmd). The `deadband` suppresses
low-confidence `argmax` flips that fragment long tracts — a precision/recall dial a
fixed hard segmenter does not expose. This is the object a downstream tract-length /
admixture-pulse dating analysis consumes.

## Public API map

The top-level package re-exports a curated surface and a set of grouped namespaces.
The submodules are all importable directly too (e.g. `tslai.pruning`), but the table
below is the intended entry map.

### Curated top-level

| Symbol | Module | Role |
|:--|:--|:--|
| [`paint`](../api/paint.qmd) | `api` | High-level entry: fit + paint queries → `Painting` |
| [`Painting`](../api/Painting.qmd) | `api` | Result: `.posteriors` (soft), `.segments()`, `.posterior_at()` |
| [`fit`](../api/fit.qmd) | `em` | The blocked-EM fit directly → `FitResult` |
| [`FitResult`](../api/FitResult.qmd) | `em` | Fitted `(Q, π, {w_i}, loglik_history)` |
| [`posterior_table`](../api/output.posterior_table.qmd) | `output` | Down-pass posterior → per-sample `Segment` tracks |
| [`hard_segments`](../api/output.hard_segments.qmd) | `output` | Collapse soft track → hard `(left, right, state)` tracts |
| [`Segment`](../api/output.Segment.qmd) | `output` | One span carrying a soft posterior + info-status |
| `INFORMATIVE`, `MISSING_INFO` | `output` | Segment status constants (missing-info ≠ uncertain) |
| [`make_generator_2state`](../api/make_generator_2state.qmd) | `model` | Build the 2-state CTMC generator `Q` |
| [`simulate_admixture`](../api/simulate_admixture.qmd) | `sim` | msprime admixture sim with known truth |
| [`local_ancestry_truth`](../api/local_ancestry_truth.qmd) | `sim` | Census-event ground-truth tracts |
| `SOURCE_A`, `SOURCE_B`, `ADMIXED` | `sim` | Population-name constants for the sim |

### Namespaces

| Namespace | Backing module(s) | Key members |
|:--|:--|:--|
| `tslai.metrics` | `validate` | [`balanced_accuracy`](../api/validate.balanced_accuracy.qmd), [`per_base_accuracy`](../api/validate.per_base_accuracy.qmd), [`mean_confidence`](../api/validate.mean_confidence.qmd), [`reliability_curve`](../api/validate.reliability_curve.qmd), [`breakpoint_flicker`](../api/validate.breakpoint_flicker.qmd), [`tract_boundary_error`](../api/validate.tract_boundary_error.qmd), [`map_truth`](../api/validate.map_truth.qmd) |
| `tslai.compare` | `compare`, `io_rfmix` | [`tslai_paint`](../api/compare.tslai_paint.qmd), [`nearest_reference_paint`](../api/compare.nearest_reference_paint.qmd), [`rfmix_paint`](../api/compare.rfmix_paint.qmd), [`head_to_head`](../api/compare.head_to_head.qmd), [`score_painter`](../api/compare.score_painter.qmd) |
| `tslai.io` | `io_tsinfer`, `io_singer` | [`infer_tree_sequence`](../api/io.infer_tree_sequence.qmd), [`add_mutations`](../api/io.add_mutations.qmd), [`singer_tree_sequences`](../api/io.singer_tree_sequences.qmd), [`write_haploid_vcf`](../api/io.write_haploid_vcf.qmd) |
| `tslai.bp` | `bp.horizontal` | [`bp_paint`](../api/bp.bp_paint.qmd), [`bp_smooth`](../api/bp.bp_smooth.qmd), [`bp_smooth_track`](../api/bp.bp_smooth_track.qmd) |
| `tslai.experiments` | `experiments` | [`admixture_experiment`](../api/experiments.admixture_experiment.qmd), [`age_sweep`](../api/experiments.age_sweep.qmd), [`scaling_sweep`](../api/experiments.scaling_sweep.qmd), [`fragmentation_experiment`](../api/experiments.fragmentation_experiment.qmd), [`arg_ensemble_experiment`](../api/experiments.arg_ensemble_experiment.qmd), [`singer_ensemble_experiment`](../api/experiments.singer_ensemble_experiment.qmd), [`flicker_vs_true_boundaries`](../api/experiments.flicker_vs_true_boundaries.qmd) |

`tslai.metrics` is an alias for the `validate` module (`from . import validate as
metrics`); `tslai.validate` works too.

## Front ends (`tslai.io`)

`tslai` is **front-end agnostic**: it paints any tree sequence with cross-tree
node-ID stability (§5). The supported routes to one:

| Front end | Module | What it does |
|:--|:--|:--|
| **tsinfer** | [`io_tsinfer`](../api/io.infer_tree_sequence.qmd) | `infer_tree_sequence` runs tsinfer on genotypes → a point-estimate ARG with native node-ID stability. `add_mutations` overlays variants on a bare ARG first. The realistic substrate where tree accuracy becomes the binding constraint (§9). |
| **SINGER** | [`io_singer`](../api/io.singer_tree_sequences.qmd) | `singer_tree_sequences` shells out to the SINGER binary (haploid VCF → `singer -ploidy 1`, with a retry loop → tskit), returning **posterior ARG samples**. These represent genuine ARG uncertainty, so they are the input to the ensemble merge (§7.4). Sample order preserved; times in generations. |
| **Relate `--compress`** | external (`io_relate`) | Convert outside Python with `Convert --mode ConvertToTreeSequence --compress`, then `tskit.load`. `--compress` is **load-bearing** — it unifies a persistent clade into one long-span node ID (§5), the invariant the edge-blocking depends on. |

RFMix is **not** a front end — it is genotype-native and appears as a *comparator*
(below).

## Comparators (`tslai.compare`)

A **painter** is any `f(ts, labels, queries) -> {sample: [Segment]}`. The harness
scores every painter against the same simulated truth on equal footing, so `tslai`,
simple baselines, and external tools compare directly.

| Painter | Module | What it is |
|:--|:--|:--|
| [`tslai_paint`](../api/compare.tslai_paint.qmd) | `compare` | The full method (EM fit + down-pass posterior) in painter form. |
| [`nearest_reference_paint`](../api/compare.nearest_reference_paint.qmd) | `compare` | ARG-native **baseline**: paint each query by its nearest labelled reference (smallest TMRCA) per tree. No CTMC, no EM — the genealogy painter the generative model should beat. |
| [`rfmix_paint`](../api/compare.rfmix_paint.qmd) | `io_rfmix` | The field-standard **segment incumbent** (RFMix v2): writes phased VCFs, shells out to `rfmix` (isolated `compare` pixi env), parses the `.fb.tsv` posteriors back to `Segment` tracks. |
| [`head_to_head`](../api/compare.head_to_head.qmd) | `compare` | Driver: simulate one admixture scenario, score a panel of painters across ARG substrates (`"true"`, `"tsinfer"`). |
| [`score_painter`](../api/compare.score_painter.qmd) | `compare` | Run one painter and return balanced accuracy, accuracy, and mean confidence. |

To add a comparator (MOSAIC/FLARE, ARGMix, AncestralPaths, …), implement the same
painter signature — shell out to the tool over a VCF, parse its calls into
`Segment`s — and pass it to `head_to_head`; it is then scored like the rest.

## Invariants that keep it correct

These come straight from the tskit data model and the method's design (§4, §12). A
naive traversal violates them and **silently** corrupts the sufficient statistics or
the painting — they are the load-bearing rules, not optional polish.

| Invariant | Where enforced | Why |
|:--|:--|:--|
| **Accumulate per edge, span-weighted, banked once on entry** | `accumulate.py` (`edges_in` loop, `w_edge = e.right − e.left`) | A persistent clade is one wide-span edge set; summing by span partitions the genome without double-counting and is the channel for genome-scale autocorrelation. Never sum per-(tree × branch). |
| **Prune per root** | `pruning.prune_tree` (`for r in tree.roots`) | A marginal tree may be a **forest**; each root carries its own `π`. There may be no single root. |
| **Skip root branches** | `accumulate.py` / `pruning.py` (root has no parent edge); `branch_kernel` returns `None` for `t ≤ 0` | Root branch length is 0 by definition in tskit; root state enters via `π` (`S_root`) only, never via `Q` on a (nonexistent) root edge. |
| **Polytomy-safe traversal** | `pruning.py` (`tree.children(u)` / sib pointers; product over all children) | Internal nodes with >2 children are real and first-class; never assume binarity. |
| **Isolated span = missing-info ≠ uncertain** | `pruning.py` (tags `missing_info`), `output.py` (`MISSING_INFO` status, `missing_info_mask`) | An isolated sample's posterior falls back to the prior and is tagged distinctly; conflating "no information" with "50–50" is a real interpretive error for introgression mapping. |
| **Keep a hard-clamped anchor set** | `em.fit` (raises `ValueError` if `soft_refs` covers every labelled tip) | Never let the whole panel float — that reintroduces unsupervised degeneracy (label switching, Q/`w` trade-off, collapse). |
| **Input must have cross-tree node-ID stability** | front ends (`tslai.io`); diagnostics (`diagnostics.node_persistence`) | The edge-blocking and horizontal coupling depend on a persistent clade being *one* node ID across trees. Delivered natively by msprime/tsinfer/SINGER, or by Relate `--compress`. Verify with the persistence histogram before trusting results. |
| **Leave-one-out for credibility** | `pruning.py` (`loo`), `accumulate.py` (`S_cred`) | Judge a tip's label against what the *rest* of the tree says, not its own self-confirming posterior. |

## Module reference

| Module | Responsibility |
|:--|:--|
| `api.py` | High-level [`paint`](../api/paint.qmd) pipeline and the [`Painting`](../api/Painting.qmd) result. The one call most users need. |
| `model.py` | Ancestry CTMC: generator `Q`, root frequencies `π`, transition matrices, tip/query emissions, the soft-credibility noise model. Generator-agnostic; touches no tskit. |
| `pruning.py` | Felsenstein two-pass sum-product per marginal tree, **per root**, polytomy-safe. Produces `γ`, `ξ`, leave-one-out marginals, missing-info tags, exact log-likelihood. The E-step kernel. |
| `branch_stats.py` | Per-branch endpoint-conditioned expected dwell times and jump counts via the Van Loan block exponential. Length-cached. The designated **Phasic seam**. |
| `accumulate.py` | The correctness core: `edge_diffs` sweep, edge-blocked **span-weighted** sufficient statistics, banked once per edge. |
| `em.py` | EM orchestration ([`fit`](../api/fit.qmd)) + the closed-form M-step (`m_step_Q/_pi/_w`) + `build_emissions`. |
| `output.py` | The deliverable: per-haplotype, per-position posterior as `Segment` tracks; `hard_segments` collapse; `missing_info_mask`; `posterior_at`. |
| `bp/horizontal.py` | Deferred §7 horizontal coupling: single-pass genome-axis forward–backward (EP) over per-tree beliefs; `bp_paint`, `bp_smooth`, `bp_smooth_track`. Helps on inferred ARGs. |
| `ensemble.py` | Marginalise ARG uncertainty: average per-position paintings across a posterior ensemble (e.g. SINGER) → `MergedSegment`s with a spread band. |
| `io_tsinfer.py` | tsinfer front end: `add_mutations`, `infer_tree_sequence`. |
| `io_singer.py` | SINGER front end: haploid VCF → posterior ARG samples; `write_haploid_vcf`, `singer_tree_sequences`. |
| `io_rfmix.py` | RFMix comparator (genotype-native): VCF/map writers, `run_rfmix`, `.fb.tsv`/`.msp.tsv` parsers, `rfmix_paint`. |
| `io_relate.py` | Relate `--compress` → tskit wrappers/checks (external C++ toolchain). |
| `io.py` | The `tslai.io` namespace — gathers the front ends. |
| `compare.py` | Head-to-head harness: painters (`tslai_paint`, `nearest_reference_paint`, `rfmix_paint`), `score_painter`, `head_to_head`. |
| `validate.py` | Metrics (exposed as `tslai.metrics`): accuracy, calibration, flicker, tract-boundary error, fragmentation, `map_truth`. |
| `sim.py` | msprime admixture sims with **census-event** ground truth; `simulate_admixture`, `local_ancestry_truth`, `admixture_demography`. |
| `ranked.py` | Order-only (dense-ranked) ablation (`ranked_tree_sequence`). **Measured not beneficial** (§6); kept runnable behind `tslai_paint(..., ranked=True)`. |
| `diagnostics.py` | Front-end-agnostic premise checks: node-persistence histogram and edge-span summary — the **go/no-go** on the central premise (§5.1). |
| `experiments.py` | End-to-end benchmark drivers (`tslai.experiments`): sim → fit → paint → score, age/scaling sweeps, ensemble and SINGER drivers, the flicker metric. |

## See also

- [API reference](../api/index.qmd) — full signatures and parameter docs.
- [`CLAUDE.md`](https://github.com/munch-group/tslai/blob/main/CLAUDE.md) — the
  authoritative spec (model, math, tree-sequence accounting, validation plan), with
  the §N anchors referenced throughout this page.
